task_completion.description: Load the three kinase structures and structurally superimpose them.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PyMOL object panel should list 1IEP, 1M17, and 1FIN. The 3D viewer should show the three protein structures overlapping, indicating they have been structurally aligned.

task_completion.description: Show ligands as spheres and DFG motifs as sticks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the PyMOL viewer, the bound ligands (STI for ABL, AQ4 for EGFR, ATP for CDK2) must be rendered as spheres. The DFG motif residues for the kinases must be rendered as sticks.

task_completion.description: Color the ABL binding pocket residues based on conservation (blue for conserved, red for divergent).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The residues forming the binding pocket on the ABL structure (1IEP) should be distinctly colored in blue and red, differentiating them from the rest of the protein structure.

task_completion.description: Measure distances between gatekeeper and DFG-Phe, and between catalytic Lys and C-helix Glu for each kinase.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PyMOL viewer or object panel should show distance measurement objects (typically dashed lines with numbers) connecting the respective residues (Gatekeeper to DFG-Phe, and Lys to Glu) across the structures.

task_completion.description: Save a ray-traced figure of the superimposed kinases at 2400x1800 resolution.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal or PyMOL command line showing the `png` command with `ray=1` (or width/height parameters 2400, 1800) saving to `~/PyMOL_Data/images/kinase_selectivity.png`, or a file manager/image viewer displaying the successfully saved high-resolution image.

task_completion.description: Write a comprehensive report with RMSDs, conservation, distances, and structural rationale.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., nano, vim, VS Code) or terminal output showing the contents of `~/PyMOL_Data/kinase_selectivity_report.txt`. The text must include pairwise RMSD values, pocket conservation status, gatekeeper/DFG analysis, distance measurements, and a rationale for imatinib selectivity.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used PyMOL to perform the structural analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the PyMOL GUI or PyMOL command-line interface actively being used to load, align, and manipulate the structures, rather than the agent just writing a script without executing it.

integrity.description: Verify the agent extracted measurements from PyMOL rather than hallucinating or copying from external knowledge.
integrity.visual_evidence: Distance measurement objects and alignment RMSD outputs should be visible in the PyMOL viewer or PyMOL text log, proving the agent calculated them in the software.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actively created the report file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should be seen using a text editor or shell commands (like echo/cat) to write the specific findings into `kinase_selectivity_report.txt`.

privileged_info_for_vlm: PDBs: 1IEP (ABL), 1M17 (EGFR), 1FIN (CDK2). CRITICAL: 1M17 has a -24 residue offset in its numbering. 1IEP: Gatekeeper T315, DFG D381-F382-G383 (DFG-OUT), Lys K271, Glu E286. Distances: K-E 3.10A, GK-DFG 11.73A. 1M17: Gatekeeper T766, DFG D831-F832-G833 (DFG-IN), Lys K721, Glu E738. Distances: K-E 3.56A, GK-DFG 12.71A. 1FIN: Gatekeeper F80, DFG D145-F146-G147 (DFG-IN), Lys K33, Glu E51. Distances: K-E 3.58A, GK-DFG 12.06A. RMSDs: ABL-EGFR ~1.19A, ABL-CDK2 ~1.35A, EGFR-CDK2 ~1.71A. Figure path: ~/PyMOL_Data/images/kinase_selectivity.png. Report path: ~/PyMOL_Data/kinase_selectivity_report.txt.
